name: notion-client (Official Notion Python SDK)
pricing_notes: Free for personal; Team plan from $10/user/mo for integrations at scale

auth.notes: Internal integration token or OAuth for third-party apps

performance.notes: REST API; 3 req/s rate limit; pagination for large databases

agent_readiness.notes: Official Notion Python SDK. from notion_client import Client. notion = Client(auth='secret_...'). notion.databases.query(database_id='...', filter={'property': 'Status', 'select': {'equals': 'Done'}}). notion.pages.create(parent={'database_id': db_id}, properties={'Name': {'title': [{'text': {'content': 'Page'}}]}}). notion.blocks.children.append(block_id, children=[text_block]). Pagination: notion.databases.query(start_cursor=cursor). Async: AsyncClient. Rate limit: 3 req/s. Block types: paragraph, heading, bulleted_list, todo, toggle, code, callout. No official webhooks.

gotchas: Verbose JSON structure for creating/updating blocks and properties
3 requests/second rate limit limits bulk operations
Property types have different structures (title, rich_text, number, select differ)
No webhooks — must poll for changes
best_when: Reading Notion databases as data sources for Python scripts
Building Notion integrations for team workflows
Content management with Notion as lightweight CMS
avoid_when: High-volume data storage (Airtable or Supabase better for data)
summary: notion-client is the Python SDK for Notion's API. notion = Client(auth='secret_...'). notion.databases.query(db_id, filter={...}) with pagination. notion.pages.create(parent, properties). notion.blocks.children.append() for content. Async via AsyncClient. 3 req/s rate limit. Verbose JSON structure for blocks. No webhooks — polling required. Best for reading Notion databases as data sources and automating content creation. MIT license.
